en: Must we not admit that we are already deprived of the most natural and most beautiful of all satisfactions, since we can no longer contemplate the arrival of our own seed, the "blessing" of children, with unmixed pleasure? Marriages where no artifices are resorted to are rare. Is this not an all-important departure from the joys which Mother Nature gave her first-born sons? Can such a state of affairs bring satisfaction? Note how men slink to their work, watch their faces at an early morning hour in the tram-cars. One of them makes his little wheels, and another writes trivial things which do not interest him. What wonder is it if such men belong to as many clubs as there are days in the week, and that among women little societies flourish, where they pour out on some particular hero or cause those unsatisfied desires which the man dulls at his restaurant or club, imbibing beer and playing at being important? To these sources of dissatisfaction is added a more serious factor. Nature has provided defenceless, weaponless man with a great amount of energy to enable him not merely to bear passively the grave dangers of existence, but also to conquer them. Mother Nature has equipped her son for tremendous hardships and has placed a costly premium on the overcoming of them, as Schopenhauer quite understood when he said that "happiness is really but the termination of unhappiness." Civilized people are, as a rule, shielded from the immediately pressing dangers, and they are therefore daily tempted to excess, for in man the animal always becomes rampant when he is not constrained by fierce necessity. Are we then indeed unrestrained? In what orgiastic festivals do we dispose of the surplus of vital power? Our moral views do not permit us that outlet.
zh: 我們豈不是必須承認，我們已被剝奪了所有滿足中最自然、最美麗的一種，因為我們再也無法以純粹的喜悅來凝視自己後裔的到來、即「兒女的祝福」嗎？不採用任何人工手段的婚姻實屬罕見。這難道不是對大自然母親賜予其長子之歡樂的一項至關重要的背離嗎？這樣一種狀態能帶來滿足嗎？請注意，男人們是如何偷偷摸摸地去工作，在清晨的電車上觀察他們的臉龐。其中一人轉動著他的小輪子，另一人則書寫著一些他自己也不感興趣的瑣碎事物。如果這樣的男人每週有多少天就參加多少個俱樂部，而女人之間則盛行著各種小團體，她們在其中將那些男人在餐館或俱樂部裡藉著喝啤酒、裝模作樣而麻痺的未滿足慾望，傾注在某個特定的英雄或事業上，這又有什麼奇怪的呢？在這些不滿的根源之外，還添加了一個更嚴重的因素。大自然為毫無防備、手無寸鐵的人類配備了大量的精力，使他不僅能消極承受生存的嚴重危險，更能征服它們。大自然母親為她的兒子裝備了應付巨大艱難的能力，並為克服這些艱難設置了高昂的獎賞，正如叔本華所充分理解的，他說：「幸福其實不過是不幸的終結。」文明人通常受到保護，免於迫在眉睫的危險，因此他們每天都受到放縱的誘惑，因為人裡面的獸性，在沒有嚴峻必要性約束時，總是會變得猖獗。那麼，我們真的毫無節制嗎？我們是在什麼狂歡的節日中，消耗掉那過剩的生命力呢？我們的道德觀念不允許我們有那樣的出口。
